API docs by Redocly

Customer Management API (0.0.1)

Download OpenAPI specification:

License:

APIs for the creation and management of customer information

Billing Groups

CREATE

Creates a new Billing Group within the specified Customer. A billing group defines how billing, invoicing, charges, and fees are organised for one or more Legal Entities belonging to the customer.

Purpose

Billing Groups act as logical containers that determine:

  • How billing rules are applied
  • Which Legal Entities/Products participate in a given billing arrangement
  • How charges and usage are aggregated
  • How invoices are grouped

They provide a structural layer between Legal Entities and billing logic, enabling more complex arrangements such as multi-entity billing, consolidated invoicing, or separate billing streams within the same customer.

Lifecycle & Versions

Newly created Billing Groups start with:

  • draft version = 0
  • submitted version = 0
  • approved version = 0

The provided payload populates the draft slot.

If submit=true is provided:

  • The draft data is also copied to the submitted slot.
  • The Billing Group is immediately submitted for evaluation.

If submit is omitted or false:

  • The caller may submit the Billing Group later via the dedicated submit endpoint.

Evaluation may return:

  • Missing configuration
  • Inconsistent or invalid billing relationships
  • Document or data requirements

Once all findings are resolved and re-evaluated, the approved version is updated and used across billing processes.

Validation & Authorisation

  • Requires a valid JWT with the CREATE_BILLING_GROUP permission.
  • The referenced customer must exist.
  • The request body is validated; errors are returned in the response.

Result

Returns the persisted Billing Group as an EntityResponse with a 201 Created status code.

Authorizations:
oauth2
path Parameters
customer_id
required
string

Id of the customer for the operation

query Parameters
submit
required
boolean

flag to indicate if the billing group should be immediately submitted

Request Body schema: application/json
required
billing_bank_account_id
required
string

Bank account to be used for billing

description
string or null
Default: "Used to group together billing for services"

Description for the billing group

legal_entity_id
required
string

Legal entity to be used for billing

name
string or null
Default: "Example billing group"

Name used to identify the billing group

Responses

evaluation service

https://tempurl.com/v1/customers/{customer_id}/billing-groups

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
application/json
{
  • "billing_bank_account_id": "string",
  • "description": "Used to group together billing for services",
  • "legal_entity_id": "string",
  • "name": "Example billing group"
}

Response samples

  • 201
Content type
application/json
{
  • "customer": "string",
  • "applied": {
    • "billing_bank_account_id": "string",
    • "description": "string",
    • "legal_entity_id": "string",
    • "name": "string"
    },
  • "applied_version": 0,
  • "created": "string",
  • "created_by": "string",
  • "draft": {
    • "billing_bank_account_id": "string",
    • "description": "string",
    • "legal_entity_id": "string",
    • "name": "string"
    },
  • "draft_version": 0,
  • "id": "string",
  • "issues": [
    • {
      • "context": "string",
      • "details": {
        • "property1": "string",
        • "property2": "string"
        },
      • "kind": "DocumentRequired",
      • "message": "string"
      }
    ],
  • "state": "New",
  • "submitted": {
    • "billing_bank_account_id": "string",
    • "description": "string",
    • "legal_entity_id": "string",
    • "name": "string"
    },
  • "submitted_version": 0,
  • "updated": "string",
  • "updated_by": "string"
}

GET

Retrieves a specific Billing Group by ID.

Purpose

Display the current draft/submitted/approved state for a single billing group prior to updates or submission.

Behaviour

  • Requires READ_BILLING_GROUP.
  • Returns 404 when the billing group is not found or not visible to caller.
  • Includes version/status context in EntityResponse<BillingGroup>.
Authorizations:
oauth2
path Parameters
customer_id
required
string

Id of the customer for the operation

entity_id
required
string

Id of the Billing Group

Responses

evaluation service

https://tempurl.com/v1/customers/{customer_id}/billing-groups/{entity_id}

Response samples

  • 200
Content type
application/json
{
  • "customer": "string",
  • "applied": {

    • "description": "string",
    • "legal_entity_id": "string",
    • "name": "string"
    },
  • "applied_version": 0,
  • "created": "string",
  • "created_by": "string",
  • "draft": {
    • "billing_bank_account_id": "string",
    • "description": "string",
    • "legal_entity_id": "string",
    • "name": "string"
    },
  • "draft_version": 0,
  • "id": "string",

      • "context": "string",
      • "details": {
        • "property1": "string",

      • "kind": "DocumentRequired",

  • "submitted": {

    • "description": "string",
    • "legal_entity_id": "string",
    • "name": "string"
    },
  • "submitted_version": 0,
  • "updated": "string",
  • "updated_by": "string"
}

UPDATE

Updates an existing Billing Group draft.

Purpose

Refine billing configuration before submission or after evaluation feedback (e.g., invoice grouping, charging rules, entity participation).

Behaviour

  • Requires UPDATE_BLLING_GROUP.
  • Uses optimistic concurrency via EntityUpdateRequest.version.
  • Optional submit flag copies changes to the submitted slot and triggers evaluation.
  • Authorisation scoped to the customer.
Authorizations:
oauth2
path Parameters
customer_id
required
string

Id of the customer for the operation

entity_id
required
string

Id of the item to be updated

query Parameters
submit
required
boolean

flag to indicate if the billing group should be immediately submitted

Request Body schema: application/json
required
billing_bank_account_id
string or null
description
string or null
Default: "Used to group together billing for services"
legal_entity_id
string or null
name
string or null
Default: "Example billing group"

Responses

evaluation service

https://tempurl.com/v1/customers/{customer_id}/billing-groups/{entity_id}

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
application/json
{
  • "billing_bank_account_id": "string",
  • "description": "Used to group together billing for services",
  • "legal_entity_id": "string",
  • "name": "Example billing group"
}

Response samples

  • 200
Content type
application/json
{
  • "count": 0,
  • "version": 0
}

DELETE

Deletes an existing Billing Group within the specified customer.

Purpose

Remove erroneous or obsolete billing arrangements prior to activation or usage.

Behaviour

  • Requires DELETE_BILLING_GROUP.
  • Returns 200 on successful deletion; 404 if the record does not exist.
  • Authorisation is scoped to the customer.
Authorizations:
oauth2
path Parameters
customer_id
required
string

Id of the customer for the operation

entity_id
required
string

Id of the Billing Group

Responses

evaluation service

https://tempurl.com/v1/customers/{customer_id}/billing-groups/{entity_id}

SUBMIT

Submits the current draft of a Billing Group for evaluation.

Purpose

Validate billing configuration (associations, charge rules, invoicing logic) before it is applied for live billing.

Behaviour

  • Requires SUBMIT_BILLING_GROUP.
  • Copies the draft data to the submitted slot; approved data is unchanged until evaluation/apply completes.
  • Returns 202 Accepted with submission metadata.
  • Authorisation scoped to the customer.
Authorizations:
oauth2
path Parameters
customer_id
required
string

Id of the customer for the operation

entity_id
required
string

Id of the billing group for the operation

Request Body schema: application/json
required

Details about the draft that should be submitted for approval.

draft_version
required
integer <int64>

Draft version that the caller expects to submit.

reason
string or null

Optional reason describing why the submission is being made.

Responses

evaluation service

https://tempurl.com/v1/customers/{customer_id}/billing-groups/{entity_id}/submit

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
application/json
{
  • "draft_version": 0,
  • "reason": "string"
}

Response samples

  • 202
Content type
application/json
{
  • "entity_id": "string",
  • "submitted_version": 0
}

TOUCH

Triggers re-evaluation for the specified Billing Group.

Purpose

Manually start the evaluation workflow (e.g., after external changes) even without new draft submissions.

Behaviour

  • Requires EVALUATE_BILLING_GROUP.
  • Invokes service-level evaluation for the customer context.
  • Returns 200 once the evaluation request is queued/processed.
Authorizations:
oauth2
path Parameters
customer_id
required
string

Id of the customer for the operation

entity_id
required
string

Id of the entity for the operation

Responses

evaluation service

https://tempurl.com/v1/customers/{customer_id}/billing-groups/{entity_id}/touch

LIST

Returns the paginated collection of Billing Groups for a customer.

Purpose

Discover existing billing arrangements before selecting a specific billing group for editing, submission, or association to legal entities/products.

Behaviour

  • Requires READ_BILLING_GROUP.
  • Paginates via PaginationSettings.
  • Scopes results to the provided customer_id.
Authorizations:
oauth2
path Parameters
customer_id
required
string or null

Id of the customer for the operation

query Parameters
page
integer or null <int64>

The page of items to be returned. Idisplayrovided 0 is assumed.

size
integer or null <int64>

The maximum number of items in a page that will be returned.

sort
string or null

The fields by which the returned data will be sorted. Specified as a csv list of fields. See endpoint description for details of supported fields for sorting.

order
string or null

The sort order for the returned data. Can be either ASC for ascending or DESC for descending.

start
string or null

Cursor for pagination; typically encoded as a string understood by the endpoint.

Responses

evaluation service

https://tempurl.com/v1/customers/{customer_id}/billing-groupss

Response samples

  • 200
Content type
application/json
[
  • {
    • "customer": "string",
    • "applied": {
      • "billing_bank_account_id": "string",
      • "description": "string",
      • "legal_entity_id": "string",
      • "name": "string"
      },
    • "applied_version": 0,
    • "created": "string",
    • "created_by": "string",
    • "draft": {
      • "billing_bank_account_id": "string",
      • "description": "string",
      • "legal_entity_id": "string",
      • "name": "string"
      },
    • "draft_version": 0,
    • "id": "string",
    • "issues": [
      • {
        • "context": "string",
        • "details": {
          • "property1": "string",
          • "property2": "string"
          },
        • "kind": "DocumentRequired",
        • "message": "string"
        }
      ],
    • "state": "New",
    • "submitted": {
      • "billing_bank_account_id": "string",
      • "description": "string",
      • "legal_entity_id": "string",
      • "name": "string"
      },
    • "submitted_version": 0,
    • "updated": "string",
    • "updated_by": "string"
    }
]

Contacts

LIST

Returns the paginated collection of Contacts for a customer.

Purpose

Discover existing contacts before selecting one for update, submission, or association to legal entities and workflows.

Behaviour

  • Requires READ_CONTACT.
  • Paginates via PaginationSettings.
  • Scopes results to the provided customer_id.

oauth2
path Parameters
customer_id
required
string or null

Id of the customer for the operation

query Parameters
page
integer or null <int64>

The page of items to be returned. Idisplayrovided 0 is assumed.

size
integer or null <int64>

The maximum number of items in a page that will be returned.

sort
string or null

The fields by which the returned data will be sorted. Specified as a csv list of fields. See endpoint description for details of supported fields for sorting.

order
string or null

The sort order for the returned data. Can be either ASC for ascending or DESC for descending.

start
string or null

Cursor for pagination; typically encoded as a string understood by the endpoint.

Responses

evaluation service

https://tempurl.com/v1/customers/{customer_id}/contacts

    • "updated_by": "string"
    }
]

CREATE

Creates a new Contact for the specified Customer. A contact represents an individual associated with the customer or its underlying legal entities, typically used for communication, operational coordination, and compliance-related processes.

Purpose

Contacts provide a structured, versioned record of:

  • Key individuals linked to the customer
  • Their roles, responsibilities, and relationship to the business
  • Points of communication for operational, billing, or compliance workflows

Contacts are an important supporting entity, enabling downstream processes such as onboarding, risk evaluation, customer servicing, and regulatory checks.

Lifecycle & Versions

Newly created contacts follow the standard entity lifecycle:

  • draft, submitted, and approved version numbers all begin at 0
  • The draft copy is populated with the data provided in the request

If submit=true is provided:

  • The draft copy is also written to the submitted slot
  • The contact is immediately submitted for evaluation

If omitted:

  • The contact remains in draft form until explicitly submitted via the submit endpoint

The approved slot is updated only once evaluation completes and the apply workflow has run successfully.

Submission & Evaluation

During evaluation the system may identify:

  • Missing or invalid personal details
  • Required supporting information
  • Policy or compliance checks triggered by the data

Any findings are returned to the caller and must be resolved before the contact can be approved.

Validation & Authorisation

  • Caller must have CREATE_CONTACT permission for the target customer.
  • A valid JWT from the authorisation server must accompany the request.
  • Payload validation errors are returned with structured details.

Result

Returns the newly created Contact wrapped in EntityResponse with a 201 Created response.

Authorizations:
oauth2
path Parameters
customer_id
required
string

Id of the customer for the operation

query Parameters
submit
required
boolean

flag to indicate if the contact should be immediately submitted

Request Body schema: application/json
required
alt_telephone
string or null

Alternative number

alt_telephone_code
string or null

Alternative number dialling code

email
string or null

Email address

name
string or null

Name associated with the contact

null or Address (object)
telephone
string or null

Primay telephone number

telephone_code
string or null

Primary telephone numbers dialling code

Responses

evaluation service

https://tempurl.com/v1/customers/{customer_id}/contacts

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
application/json
{
  • "alt_telephone": "string",
  • "alt_telephone_code": "string",
  • "email": "string",
  • "name": "string",
  • "postal_address": { },
  • "telephone": "string",
  • "telephone_code": "string"
}

  • "submitted_version": 0,

GET

Retrieves a specific Contact by ID.

Purpose

Display the current draft/submitted/approved state for a single contact before editing or submission.

Behaviour

  • Requires READ_CONTACT.
  • Returns 404 when the contact is not found or not visible to caller.
  • Includes version/status context in EntityResponse<Contact>.
Authorizations:
oauth2
path Parameters
customer_id
required
string

Id of the customer for the operation

entity_id
required
string

Id of the Contact

Responses

evaluation service

https://tempurl.com/v1/customers/{customer_id}/contacts/{entity_id}

UPDATE

Updates an existing Contact draft.

Purpose

Refine contact details (roles, communication info, responsibilities) before submission or after evaluation feedback.

Behaviour

  • Requires UPDATE_CONTACT.
  • Uses optimistic concurrency via EntityUpdateRequest.version.
  • Optional submit flag copies the change to the submitted slot and triggers evaluation.
  • Authorisation scoped to the customer.
Authorizations:
oauth2
path Parameters
customer_id
required
string

Id of the customer for the operation

entity_id
required
string

Id of the item to be updated

query Parameters
submit
required
boolean

flag to indicate if the contact change should be immediately submitted

Request Body schema: application/json
required

Versioned update of the Contact details.

required
object

Provides the details used to update an existing contact.

version
required
integer <int64>

The version of the item to be updated. If this does not match the current version on record then an error will be returned.

Responses

evaluation service

https://tempurl.com/v1/customers/{customer_id}/contacts/{entity_id}

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
application/json
{
  • "document": {
    • "alt_telephone": "string",
    • "alt_telephone_code": "string",
    • "email": "contact-email@example.com",
    • "name": "Example name",
    • "postal_address": { },
    • "telephone": "55512345678",
    • "telephone_code": "+44"
    },
  • "version": 0
}

Response samples

  • 200
Content type
application/json
{
  • "count": 0,
  • "version": 0
}

DELETE

Deletes an existing Contact within the specified customer.

Purpose

Remove erroneous or obsolete contact records prior to activation or use in workflows.

Behaviour

  • Requires DELETE_CONTACT.
  • Returns 200 on successful deletion; 404 if the record does not exist.
  • Authorisation scoped to the customer.
Authorizations:
oauth2
path Parameters
customer_id
required
string

Id of the customer for the operation

entity_id
required
string

Id of the Contact

Responses

evaluation service

https://tempurl.com/v1/customers/{customer_id}/contacts/{entity_id}

SUBMIT

Submits the current draft of a Contact for evaluation.

Purpose

Validate contact information and associated compliance checks before making the contact available to downstream processes.

Behaviour

  • Requires SUBMIT_CONTACT.
  • Copies the draft data to the submitted slot; approved data is unchanged until evaluation/apply completes.
  • Returns 202 Accepted with submission metadata.
  • Authorisation scoped to the customer.
Authorizations:
oauth2
path Parameters
customer_id
required
string

Id of the customer for the operation

entity_id
required
string

Id of the contact for the operation

Request Body schema: application/json
required

Details about the draft that should be submitted for approval.

draft_version
required
integer <int64>

Draft version that the caller expects to submit.

reason
string or null

Optional reason describing why the submission is being made.

Responses

evaluation service

https://tempurl.com/v1/customers/{customer_id}/contacts/{entity_id}/submit

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
application/json
{
  • "draft_version": 0,
  • "reason": "string"
}

Response samples

  • 202
Content type
application/json
{
  • "entity_id": "string",
  • "submitted_version": 0
}

TOUCH

Triggers re-evaluation for the specified Contact.

Purpose

Manually start the evaluation workflow (e.g., after external data changes) without submitting new draft content.

Behaviour

  • Requires EVALUATE_CONTACT.
  • Invokes service-level evaluation for the customer context.
  • Returns 200 once the evaluation request is queued/processed.
Authorizations:
oauth2
path Parameters
customer_id
required
string

Id of the customer for the operation

entity_id
required
string

Id of the entity for the operation

Responses

evaluation service

https://tempurl.com/v1/customers/{customer_id}/contacts/{entity_id}/touch

Legal Entities

LIST

Returns the paginated collection of Legal Entities for a customer.

Purpose

Discover existing legal entities, typically to show summaries in dashboards or to locate a specific entity before drilling into details.

Behaviour

  • Paginates via PaginationSettings.
  • Scopes to the supplied customer_id; caller must have READ_LEGAL_ENTITY.
  • Returns lightweight EntityResponse<LegalEntity> entries.
Authorizations:
oauth2
path Parameters
customer_id
required
string or null

Id of the customer for the operation

query Parameters
page
integer or null <int64>

The page of items to be returned. Idisplayrovided 0 is assumed.

size
integer or null <int64>

The maximum number of items in a page that will be returned.

sort
string or null

The fields by which the returned data will be sorted. Specified as a csv list of fields. See endpoint description for details of supported fields for sorting.

order
string or null

The sort order for the returned data. Can be either ASC for ascending or DESC for descending.

start
string or null

Cursor for pagination; typically encoded as a string understood by the endpoint.

Responses

evaluation service

https://tempurl.com/v1/customers/{customer_id}/legal-entities

Response samples

  • 200
Content type
application/json
[
  • {
    • "customer": "string",

CREATE

Creates a new Legal Entity, the core business object representing an organisation, its ownership, operational details, and beneficial parties.

Purpose

A Legal Entity is the foundation of the merchant lifecycle. It provides the authoritative record of:

  • Business identity and structure.
  • Ownership and persons of significant control.
  • Operational characteristics.
  • Relationships to other legal entities (for complex hierarchies).

Typical Use Cases

  • Onboarding a new merchant or partner.
  • Registering a new business entity within an existing group.
  • Defining hierarchical corporate structures.

Submission & Evaluation

After creation, the Legal Entity may be submitted for approval. Submission triggers the evaluation workflow, which may identify:

  • Missing or inconsistent business information.
  • Required supporting documents.
  • Compliance or risk-related questions.

Any findings are returned to the client. Once addressed and re-evaluated, the Legal Entity can be applied, making its data active across all downstream systems.

Related Domains

Legal Entities link to several other resource domains:

  • Products.
  • Trading locations.
  • Bank accounts.

These are managed by their respective APIs but form part of the overall entity lifecycle.

Authorizations:
oauth2
path Parameters
customer_id
required
string

Id of the customer for the operation

query Parameters
submit
required
boolean

flag to indicate if the legal entity should be immediately submitted

Request Body schema: application/json
required
Array of objects or null (BusinessContact)

Contacts within the business (used if it is necessary to contact the business in order to provide support)

business_email
required
string
Default: "business-email@example.com"

Business email address

business_type
string (BusinessType)
Enum: "SoleTrader" "Partnership" "Company" "Standalone_FCA_Regulated"
goods_ownership
string (GoodsOwnership)
Enum: "Owned" "ThirdParty" "Leased" "Other"

Ownership of goods or services offered

card_turnover_per_annum
integer or null <int32> >= 0
Default: 45000

The total amount of card transaction processed by the business annually

company_registration_no
string or null
Default: "12345678"

The company registration numnber as recorded with UK companies house (not required if sole trader)

charity_registration_no
string or null
Default: "987654321"

The charity registration number as recorded with the relevant authority (not required if not a charity)

date_business_created
string or null
Default: "2025-01-01"

The date the business was created (i.e. when registered with companies house or when formed if a sole trader)

date_trading_started
string or null
Default: "2025-01-01"

The date the business started trading (if different to the date the business was formed)

null or LegalEntityStatus (string)
Default: "Active"
Array of objects or null (LegalEntityWithControl)

Details of any parent companies that have control of this business.

maximum_transaction_value
integer or null <int32> >= 0
Default: 10000

Expected maximum transaction value (this will not determine a limit and is used only for monitoring of transaction activity to protect against fraud)

merchant_category_code
string or null
Default: "1234"

The nerchant category code (MCC) for the business

name
string or null
Default: "Example business"

The name of the legal entity

Array of objects or null (PersonWithControl)

Details of the people who have control within the business. This could be a share holder or a director for example.

Array of objects or null (RegisteredAddress)

Details of the registered addresses this business has operated under. Only one address can be marked as current.

country_of_authority
string or null

Country authority if different from registered address

Array of objects or null (PartnerWithInterest)
standard_industry_classification
string or null
Default: "1234"

The Standard Industry Classification (SIC) for the business

trading_industry_classification
string or null
Default: "1234"

The Standard Industry Classification (SIC) for the business - trading

trading_name
string or null
Default: "Trading as..."

The trading name of the legal entity (if different from the legal name)

turnover_per_annum
integer or null <int32> >= 0
Default: 75000

The total annual turnover in GBP of the business

vat_registration_no
string or null
Default: "GB123456789"

Vat registration number (if VAT registered)

null or VatRegistrationStatus (string)
Default: "Registered"

Responses

evaluation service

https://tempurl.com/v1/customers/{customer_id}/legal-entities

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
application/json
{
  • "business_contacts": [
    • {
      • "contact_id": "12345678",
      • "contact_type": "Customer"
      }
    ],
  • "business_email": "business-email@example.com",
  • "business_type": "SoleTrader",
  • "goods_ownership": "Owned",

  • "company_registration_no": "12345678",
  • "charity_registration_no": "987654321",
  • "date_business_created": "2025-01-01",
  • "date_trading_started": "2025-01-01",
  • "legal_entity_status": "Active",
  • "legal_entity_with_control": [
    • {
      • "control_types": [
        • "Shareholder"
        ],
      • "legal_entity_id": "string"
      }
    ],
  • "maximum_transaction_value": 10000,
  • "merchant_category_code": "1234",
  • "name": "Example business",
  • "persons_with_control": [
    • {
      • "control_types": [
        • "Shareholder"
        ],
      • "person": {
        • "addresses": [
          • {
            • "address": {
              • "code": "AB1 2CD",
              • "country": "GB",
              • "line1": "Line 1",
              • "line2": "string",
              • "line3": "string",
              • "locality": "string",
              • "city": "string",
              • "name": "House name",
              • "region": "London"
              },
            • "current": true,
            • "date_from": "2015-01-01",
            • "date_to": "2015-01-01"
            }
          ],
        • "date_of_birth": "1980-01-01",
        • "first_name": "Mary",
        • "last_name": "Anonymous",
        • "middle_name": "Jane",
        • "nationality": "GB",
        • "title": "Ms"
        }
      }
    ],
  • "registered_addresses": [
    • {
      • "address": {
        • "code": "AB1 2CD",
        • "country": "GB",
        • "line1": "Line 1",
        • "line2": "string",
        • "line3": "string",
        • "locality": "string",
        • "city": "string",
        • "name": "House name",
        • "region": "London"
        },
      • "current": true,
      • "date_from": "2015-01-01",
      • "date_to": "2015-01-01"
      }
    ],
  • "country_of_authority": "string",
  • "partners_with_interest": [
    • {
      • "attribution_type": [
        • "Introducer"
        ],
      • "legal_entity_id": "string",
      • "descriptors": [
        • [
          • {
            • "key": "string",
            • "value": "string"
            }
          ]
        ]
      }
    ],
  • "standard_industry_classification": "1234",
  • "trading_industry_classification": "1234",
  • "trading_name": "Trading as...",
  • "turnover_per_annum": 75000,
  • "vat_registration_no": "GB123456789",
  • "vat_registration_status": "Registered"
}

            • {

  • "updated": "string",
  • "updated_by": "string"
}

GET

Retrieves a specific Legal Entity by its ID.

Purpose

Fetch the current representation of a single legal entity to display or validate data prior to updates/submission.

Behaviour

  • Enforces customer scoping and READ_LEGAL_ENTITY.
  • Returns 404 when the entity does not exist or is not visible to the caller.
Authorizations:
oauth2
path Parameters
customer_id
required
string

Id of the customer for the operation

entity_id
required
string

Id of the legal entity

Responses

evaluation service

https://tempurl.com/v1/customers/{customer_id}/legal-entities/{entity_id}

Response samples

  • 200
Content type
application/json
{
  • "customer": "string",

UPDATE

Updates an existing Legal Entity draft (business data).

Purpose

Apply changes to core legal-entity attributes before submission or as part of remediation after evaluation feedback.

Behaviour

  • Requires UPDATE_LEGAL_ENTITY.
  • Supports optional immediate submission via submit query flag.
  • Uses optimistic concurrency via version inside EntityUpdateRequest.
Authorizations:
oauth2
path Parameters
customer_id
required
string

Id of the customer for the operation

entity_id
required
string

Id of the item to be updated

query Parameters
submit
required
boolean

flag to indicate if the legal entity change should be immediately submitted

Request Body schema: application/json
required
Array of objects or null (BusinessContact)
business_email
string or null
Default: "business-email@example.com"
business_type
string or null
Default: "This is an example business created from API documentation testing"
card_turnover_per_annum
integer or null <int32> >= 0
Default: 45000
company_registration_no
string or null
Default: "12345678"
date_business_created
string or null
Default: "2025-01-01"
date_trading_started
string or null
Default: "2025-01-01"
null or LegalEntityStatus (string)
Default: "Active"
Array of objects or null (LegalEntityWithControl)
maximum_transaction_value
integer or null <int32> >= 0
Default: 2000
merchant_category_code
string or null
Default: "1234"
name
string or null
Default: "Example business"
Array of objects or null (PersonWithControl)
Array of objects or null (RegisteredAddress)
standard_industry_classification
string or null
Default: "1234"
trading_name
string or null
Default: "Trading as..."
turnover_per_annum
integer or null <int32> >= 0
Default: 75000
vat_registration_no
string or null
Default: "GB123456789"
null or VatRegistrationStatus (string)
Default: "Registered"

Responses

evaluation service

https://tempurl.com/v1/customers/{customer_id}/legal-entities/{entity_id}

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
application/json
{
  • "business_contacts": [
    • {
      • "contact_id": "12345678",
      • "contact_type": "Customer"
      }
    ],
  • "business_email": "business-email@example.com",
  • "business_type": "This is an example business created from API documentation testing",

    • {
      • "control_types": [
        • "Shareholder"
        ],
      • "legal_entity_id": "string"
      }
    ],
  • "maximum_transaction_value": 2000,
  • "merchant_category_code": "1234",
  • "name": "Example business",
  • "persons_with_control": [
    • {
      • "control_types": [
        • "Shareholder"
        ],
      • "person": {
        • "addresses": [
          • {
            • "address": {
              • "code": "AB1 2CD",
              • "country": "GB",
              • "line1": "Line 1",
              • "line2": "string",
              • "line3": "string",
              • "locality": "string",
              • "city": "string",
              • "name": "House name",
              • "region": "London"
              },
            • "current": true,
            • "date_from": "2015-01-01",
            • "date_to": "2015-01-01"
            }
          ],
        • "date_of_birth": "1980-01-01",
        • "first_name": "Mary",
        • "last_name": "Anonymous",
        • "middle_name": "Jane",
        • "nationality": "GB",
        • "title": "Ms"
        }
      }
    ],
  • "registered_addresses": [
    • {
      • "address": {
        • "code": "AB1 2CD",
        • "country": "GB",
        • "line1": "Line 1",
        • "line2": "string",
        • "line3": "string",
        • "locality": "string",
        • "city": "string",
        • "name": "House name",
        • "region": "London"
        },
      • "current": true,
      • "date_from": "2015-01-01",
      • "date_to": "2015-01-01"
      }
    ],
  • "standard_industry_classification": "1234",
  • "trading_name": "Trading as...",
  • "turnover_per_annum": 75000,
  • "vat_registration_no": "GB123456789",
  • "vat_registration_status": "Registered"
}

Response samples

  • 200
Content type
application/json
{
  • "count": 0,
  • "version": 0
}

DELETE

Soft-deletes a Legal Entity (logical delete).

Purpose

Retire an entity that was created in error or is no longer needed prior to activation.

Behaviour

  • Requires DELETE_LEGAL_ENTITY.
  • Returns 200 on success, 404 if the entity cannot be found.
Authorizations:
oauth2
path Parameters
customer_id
required
string

Id of the customer for the operation

entity_id
required
string

Id of the legal entity

Responses

evaluation service

https://tempurl.com/v1/customers/{customer_id}/legal-entities/{entity_id}

SUBMIT

Submits the current draft of a Legal Entity for evaluation.

Purpose

Trigger the compliance/risk evaluation workflow that validates completeness, required documents, and business rules.

Behaviour

  • Requires SUBMIT_LEGAL_ENTITY.
  • On acceptance, copies draft to submitted slot and starts evaluation.
  • Returns 202 Accepted with submission metadata.
Authorizations:
oauth2
path Parameters
customer_id
required
string

Id of the customer for the operation

entity_id
required
string

Id of the legal entity for the operation

Request Body schema: application/json
required

Details about the draft that should be submitted for approval.

draft_version
required
integer <int64>

Draft version that the caller expects to submit. Must be the current draft version. Used to prevent submission of stale data.

reason
string or null

Optional reason describing why the submission is being made.

Responses

evaluation service

https://tempurl.com/v1/customers/{customer_id}/legal-entities/{entity_id}/submit

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
application/json
{
  • "draft_version": 0,
  • "reason": "string"
}

Response samples

  • 202
Content type
application/json
{
  • "entity_id": "string",
  • "submitted_version": 0
}

TOUCH

Triggers evaluation for a Legal Entity without making data changes.

Purpose

Re-run downstream evaluations after external changes (e.g., document status updates) or to refresh derived state.

Behaviour

  • Requires EVALUATE_LEGAL_ENTITY.
  • Fires the same evaluation pipeline used post-submission.
Authorizations:
oauth2
path Parameters
customer_id
required
string

Id of the customer for the operation

entity_id
required
string

Id of the entity for the operation

Responses

evaluation service

https://tempurl.com/v1/customers/{customer_id}/legal-entities/{entity_id}/touch

Bank Accounts

LIST

Returns the paginated collection of Bank Accounts for a legal entity.

Purpose

Discover configured payout/settlement destinations before selecting or editing a specific bank account.

Behaviour

  • Requires READ_BANK_ACCOUNT.
  • Paginates via PaginationSettings.
  • Scopes results to the provided customer_id and legal_entity_id.
Authorizations:
oauth2
path Parameters
customer_id
required
string

Id of the customer for the operation

legal_entity_id
required
string

Id of the legal entity

query Parameters
page
integer or null <int64>

The page of items to be returned. Idisplayrovided 0 is assumed.

size
integer or null <int64>

The maximum number of items in a page that will be returned.

sort
string or null

The fields by which the returned data will be sorted. Specified as a csv list of fields. See endpoint description for details of supported fields for sorting.

order
string or null

The sort order for the returned data. Can be either ASC for ascending or DESC for descending.

start
string or null

Cursor for pagination; typically encoded as a string understood by the endpoint.

Responses

evaluation service

https://tempurl.com/v1/customers/{customer_id}/legal-entities/{legal_entity_id}/bank-accounts

Response samples

  • 200
  • 500
Content type
application/json
[
  • {
    • "customer": "string",
    • "applied": {
      • "account_holder_names": [
        • "string"
        ],
      • "account_number": "string",
      • "bank_city": "string",
      • "bank_country": "string",
      • "bank_name": "string",
      • "billing_default": true,
      • "iban": "string",
      • "legal_entity_id": "string",
      • "name": "string",
      • "sort_code": "string",
      • "swift": "string",
      • "descriptors": [
        • {
          • "key": "string",
          • "value": "string"
          }
        ],
      • "labels": [
        • "string"
        ]
      },
    • "applied_version": 0,
    • "created": "string",
    • "created_by": "string",
    • "draft": {
      • "account_holder_names": [
        • "string"
        ],
      • "account_number": "string",
      • "bank_city": "string",
      • "bank_country": "string",
      • "bank_name": "string",
      • "billing_default": true,
      • "iban": "string",
      • "legal_entity_id": "string",
      • "name": "string",
      • "sort_code": "string",
      • "swift": "string",
      • "descriptors": [
        • {
          • "key": "string",
          • "value": "string"
          }
        ],
      • "labels": [
        • "string"
        ]
      },
    • "draft_version": 0,
    • "id": "string",
    • "issues": [
      • {
        • "context": "string",
        • "details": {
          • "property1": "string",
          • "property2": "string"
          },
        • "kind": "DocumentRequired",
        • "message": "string"
        }
      ],
    • "state": "New",
    • "submitted": {
      • "account_holder_names": [
        • "string"
        ],
      • "account_number": "string",
      • "bank_city": "string",
      • "bank_country": "string",
      • "bank_name": "string",
      • "billing_default": true,
      • "iban": "string",
      • "legal_entity_id": "string",
      • "name": "string",
      • "sort_code": "string",
      • "swift": "string",
      • "descriptors": [
        • {
          • "key": "string",
          • "value": "string"
          }
        ],
      • "labels": [
        • "string"
        ]
      },
    • "submitted_version": 0,
    • "updated": "string",
    • "updated_by": "string"
    }
]

CREATE

Creates a new Bank Account for a given Legal Entity within a Customer. Bank accounts capture settlement and payout details used across downstream payment and billing processes.

Purpose

A Bank Account belongs to:

  • A Customer (top-level customer container), and
  • A Legal Entity (the business that owns/uses the bank account).

It provides the canonical record of where funds are settled and how payouts are routed for that legal entity.

Lifecycle & Versions

Bank accounts participate in the same draft / submitted / approved lifecycle as other configurable entities:

  • On creation, the draft, submitted, and approved version numbers are all initialised to 0.
  • The draft slot is always updated with the provided details.
  • The approved slot is only updated once evaluation completes successfully and the apply workflow has run.

The legal_entity_id is taken from the URL and stored on the bank account; this association cannot be changed after creation.

Submission & Evaluation

If the submit query flag is set:

  • The draft data is also copied to the submitted slot.
  • The bank account is immediately submitted for evaluation.

If submit is not set:

  • The caller is expected to submit the bank account later via the dedicated submit endpoint.
  • Bank account submission may also be triggered from a Legal Entity workflow (for example, as part of a post-apply process when the Legal Entity is approved).

During evaluation, any issues, missing data, or document requirements are surfaced back to the caller. Once these are resolved and evaluation completes, the approved copy is updated and used across all systems.

Validation & Authorisation

  • The referenced Legal Entity must exist at the time of creation.
  • The caller must be authorised to create bank accounts for the given customer and legal entity (via a signed JWT from the authorisation server with the required permissions).
  • The request payload must be valid; any validation failures are returned in the response with details of the errors.
Authorizations:
oauth2
path Parameters
customer_id
required
string

Id of the customer for the operation

legal_entity_id
required
string

Id of the legal entity

query Parameters
submit
boolean

flag to indicate if the bank account should be immediately submitted

Request Body schema: application/json
required
account_holder_names
Array of strings or null
Default: "MR A C HOLDER"

List of names of the account holders for the account

account_number
string or null
Default: "12345678"

The account number of the bank account

bank_city
string or null
Default: "London"

City of the branch of the bank at which the account is held

bank_country
string or null
Default: "GB"

Country of the bank at which the account is held

bank_name
string or null
Default: "NatWest"

Name of the bank at which the account is held

billing_default
boolean or null
Default: false

Indicator for assigning this bank account as the default bank account for billing, there must be at least one default bank account at all times. If not specified, false is assumed.

iban
string or null

International Bank Account Number (IBAN)

name
required
string
Default: "Example bank account"

A unique name used to identify the bank account within this system

sort_code
string or null
Default: "12-34-56"

Sort code of the account

swift
string or null

Swift code identifies the bank holding the account

Array of objects or null (Descriptor)

Meta-data that describes entity

labels
Array of strings or null or null

Label that can be used query customer data

Responses

evaluation service

https://tempurl.com/v1/customers/{customer_id}/legal-entities/{legal_entity_id}/bank-accounts

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
application/json
{
  • "account_holder_names": "MR A C HOLDER",
  • "account_number": "12345678",
  • "bank_city": "London",
  • "bank_country": "GB",
  • "bank_name": "NatWest",
  • "billing_default": false,
  • "iban": "string",
  • "name": "Example bank account",
  • "sort_code": "12-34-56",
  • "swift": "string",
  • "descriptors": [
    • {
      • "key": "string",
      • "value": "string"
      }
    ],
  • "labels": [
    • "string"
    ]
}

  • 201
Content type
application/json

GET

Retrieves a specific Bank Account by ID within a legal entity.

Purpose

Display the current draft/submitted/approved state for a single bank account prior to updates or submission.

Behaviour

  • Requires READ_BANK_ACCOUNT.
  • Returns 404 when the bank account is not found or not visible to caller.
  • Includes status/version details in EntityResponse<BankAccount>.
Authorizations:
oauth2
path Parameters
customer_id
required
string

Id of the customer for the operation

legal_entity_id
required
string

Id of the legal entity

entity_id
required
string

Id of the bank account

Responses

evaluation service

https://tempurl.com/v1/customers/{customer_id}/legal-entities/{legal_entity_id}/bank-accounts/{entity_id}

UPDATE

Updates an existing Bank Account draft.

Purpose

Correct or enrich payout/settlement details before submission or after evaluation feedback.

Behaviour

  • Requires UPDATE_BANK_ACCOUNT.
  • Uses optimistic concurrency via EntityUpdateRequest.version.
  • Optional submit flag copies the change to the submitted slot and triggers evaluation.
  • Scopes authorisation to the owning customer and legal entity.
Authorizations:
oauth2
path Parameters
customer_id
required
string

Id of the customer for the operation

legal_entity_id
required
string

Id of the legal entity

entity_id
required
string

Id of the item to be updated

query Parameters
submit
required
boolean

flag to indicate if the bank account change should be immediately submitted

Request Body schema: application/json
required
billing_default
boolean or null
name
string or null
Default: "Example bank account"

Responses

evaluation service

https://tempurl.com/v1/customers/{customer_id}/legal-entities/{legal_entity_id}/bank-accounts/{entity_id}

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
application/json
{
  • "billing_default": true,
  • "name": "Example bank account"
}

Response samples

  • 200
Content type
application/json
{
  • "count": 0,
  • "version": 0
}

DELETE

Deletes an existing Bank Account for the specified legal entity.

Purpose

Remove erroneous or obsolete bank account definitions prior to activation or payout routing.

Behaviour

  • Requires DELETE_BANK_ACCOUNT.
  • Returns 200 on successful deletion; 404 if the record does not exist.
  • Authorisation is scoped to the customer and legal entity.
Authorizations:
oauth2
path Parameters
customer_id
required
string

Id of the customer for the operation

legal_entity_id
required
string

Id of the legal entity

entity_id
required
string

Id of the bank account

Responses

evaluation service

https://tempurl.com/v1/customers/{customer_id}/legal-entities/{legal_entity_id}/bank-accounts/{entity_id}

SUBMIT

Submits the current draft of a Bank Account for evaluation.

Purpose

Trigger validation of payout/settlement details and compliance checks prior to activation.

Behaviour

  • Requires SUBMIT_BANK_ACCOUNT.
  • Copies the draft data to the submitted slot; approved data is unchanged until evaluation and apply complete.
  • Returns 202 Accepted with submission metadata.
  • Authorisation is scoped to the customer and legal entity provided.
Authorizations:
oauth2
path Parameters
customer_id
required
string

Id of the customer for the operation

legal_entity_id
required
string

Id of the legal entity

entity_id
required
string

Id of the bank account for the operation

Request Body schema: application/json
required

Details about the draft that should be submitted for approval.

draft_version
required
integer <int64>

Draft version that the caller expects to submit.

reason
string or null

Optional reason describing why the submission is being made.

Responses

evaluation service

https://tempurl.com/v1/customers/{customer_id}/legal-entities/{legal_entity_id}/bank-accounts/{entity_id}/submit

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
application/json
{
  • "draft_version": 0,
  • "reason": "string"
}

Response samples

  • 202
Content type
application/json
{
  • "entity_id": "string",
  • "state": "New",
  • "submitted_version": 0
}

TOUCH

Triggers a re-evaluation of the specified Bank Account.

Purpose

Manually start the evaluation workflow (e.g., after external changes) even when no new draft data has been submitted.

Behaviour

  • Requires EVALUATE_BANK_ACCOUNT.
  • Invokes service-level evaluation for the customer/legal entity context.
  • Returns 200 once the evaluation request is queued/processed.
Authorizations:
oauth2
path Parameters
customer_id
required
string

Id of the customer for the operation

legal_entity_id
required
string

Id of the legal entity

entity_id
required
string

Id of the entity for the operation

Responses

evaluation service

https://tempurl.com/v1/customers/{customer_id}/legal-entities/{legal_entity_id}/bank-accounts/{entity_id}/touch

Product Agreements

LIST

Returns the paginated collection of Product Agreements for a legal entity.

Purpose

Discover configured product agreements before selecting one for update, submission, or evaluation.

Behaviour

  • Requires READ_PRODUCT_AGREEMENT.
  • Paginates via PaginationSettings.
  • Scopes results to the provided customer and legal entity.
Authorizations:
oauth2
path Parameters
customer_id
required
string

Id of the customer for the operation

legal_entity_id
required
string

Id of the legal entity for which the products should be returned

query Parameters
page
integer or null <int64>

The page of items to be returned. Idisplayrovided 0 is assumed.

size
integer or null <int64>

The maximum number of items in a page that will be returned.

sort
string or null

The fields by which the returned data will be sorted. Specified as a csv list of fields. See endpoint description for details of supported fields for sorting.

order
string or null

The sort order for the returned data. Can be either ASC for ascending or DESC for descending.

start
string or null

Cursor for pagination; typically encoded as a string understood by the endpoint.

Responses

evaluation service

https://tempurl.com/v1/customers/{customer_id}/legal-entities/{legal_entity_id}/product-agreements

Response samples

  • 200
Content type
application/json
[
  • {
    • "customer": "string",
    • "applied": {
      • "display_name": "string",
      • "features": [
        • "string"
        ],
      • "legal_entity_id": "string",
      • "product_configuration": null,
      • "product_type": "string",
      • "rate_card_id": "string"
      },
    • "applied_version": 0,
    • "created": "string",
    • "created_by": "string",
    • "draft": {
      • "display_name": "string",
      • "features": [
        • "string"
        ],
      • "legal_entity_id": "string",
      • "product_configuration": null,
      • "product_type": "string",
      • "rate_card_id": "string"
      },
    • "draft_version": 0,
    • "id": "string",
    • "issues": [
      • {
        • "context": "string",
        • "details": {
          • "property1": "string",
          • "property2": "string"
          },
        • "kind": "DocumentRequired",
        • "message": "string"
        }
      ],
    • "state": "New",
    • "submitted": {
      • "display_name": "string",
      • "features": [
        • "string"
        ],
      • "legal_entity_id": "string",
      • "product_configuration": null,
      • "product_type": "string",
      • "rate_card_id": "string"
      },
    • "submitted_version": 0,
    • "updated": "string",
    • "updated_by": "string"
    }
]

CREATE

Creates a new Product Agreement for the specified Customer and (typically) associated Legal Entity. A product agreement defines which products or services the customer intends to use, together with configuration, eligibility information, and any product-specific operational requirements.

Purpose

Product Agreements serve as the core artefacts for enabling and configuring products within the platform. They describe:

  • Which products a customer is onboarding to
  • The configuration and parameters for each product
  • Product-specific requirements (documents, checks, capabilities)
  • The lifecycle and state of each activated product

They act as a contract-level definition between the platform and the customer's legal entity.

Lifecycle & Versions

Product Agreements follow the standard entity lifecycle:

  • draft, submitted, and approved version numbers all begin at 0
  • The draft copy is populated using the request body

If submit=true is provided:

  • The draft is also copied to the submitted slot
  • The Product Agreement is immediately submitted for evaluation

If not submitted:

  • The agreement remains in draft form until explicitly submitted via the submit endpoint or by workflows that automatically trigger submission

The approved slot is only updated once evaluation completes and the apply workflow has been successfully executed.

Submission & Evaluation

Upon submission, a Product Agreement is evaluated using the product's definition and configured workflow logic. Evaluation may identify:

  • Missing or invalid configuration fields
  • Inconsistent product selections
  • Required documents or compliance checks
  • Product dependency issues (e.g., prerequisites not met)

Findings are returned to the caller and must be resolved before approval can occur.

Relationships

Product Agreements typically relate to:

  • A Legal Entity that the product applies to
  • One or more Product Definitions that specify rules and capabilities
  • Associated configuration entities (billing, locations, bank accounts)

These links allow workflows to determine what configuration must exist before the agreement can be approved.

Validation & Authorisation

  • Caller must present a valid JWT containing CREATE_PRODUCT_AGREEMENT or equivalent permissions for the target customer.
  • The referenced customer must exist.
  • Request payload must pass validation; structured error responses are returned for invalid data.

Result

Returns the newly created Product Agreement wrapped in an EntityResponse with a 201 Created status.

Authorizations:
oauth2
path Parameters
customer_id
required
string

Id of the customer for the operation

legal_entity_id
required
string

Id of the legal entity

query Parameters
submit
required
boolean

flag to indicate if the product agreement should be immediately submitted

Request Body schema: application/json
required

Details of the new product to be created

display_name
string or null

Display name for the product

features
required
Array of strings

list of additional features enabled for the product

product_configuration
required
any

Product Configuration - object containing product specific configuration

product_type
required
string

Product type - as defined in the product catalogue

rate_card_id
required
string

Rate card identifier

Responses

evaluation service

https://tempurl.com/v1/customers/{customer_id}/legal-entities/{legal_entity_id}/product-agreements

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
application/json
{
  • "display_name": "string",
  • "features": [
    • "string"
    ],
  • "product_configuration": null,
  • "product_type": "string",
  • "rate_card_id": "string"
}

Response samples

  • 201
Content type
application/json
{
  • "customer": "string",
  • "applied": {
    • "display_name": "string",
    • "features": [
      • "string"

Purpose

  • Uses optimistic concurrency via EntityUpdateRequest.version.
  • Optional submit flag copies the change to the submitted slot and triggers evaluation.
  • Authorisation scoped to customer and legal entity.

DELETE

Deletes an existing Product Agreement.

Purpose

Remove obsolete or erroneous agreements prior to activation or usage in downstream flows.

Behaviour

  • Requires DELETE_PRODUCT_AGREEMENT.
  • Returns 200 on successful deletion; 404 if the record does not exist.
  • Authorisation scoped to the customer and legal entity.

Purpose

Behaviour

  • Requires SUBMIT_PRODUCT_AGREEMENT.
  • Copies draft data to the submitted slot; approved data is unchanged until evaluation/apply completes.
  • Returns 202 Accepted with submission metadata.
  • Authorisation scoped to the customer and legal entity.

  • Requires EVALUATE_PRODUCT_AGREEMENT.
  • Invokes service-level evaluation for the customer/legal entity context.
  • Returns 200 once the evaluation request is queued/processed.

Trading Locations

LIST

Returns the paginated collection of Trading Locations for a legal entity.

Purpose

Discover configured operating locations before selecting one for update, submission, or evaluation.

Behaviour

  • Requires READ_TRADING_LOCATION.
  • Paginates via PaginationSettings.
  • Scopes results to the provided customer and legal entity.
Authorizations:
oauth2
path Parameters
customer_id
required
string

Id of the customer for the operation

legal_entity_id
required
string

Id of the legal entity for which the trading locations should be returned

query Parameters
page
integer or null <int64>

The page of items to be returned. Idisplayrovided 0 is assumed.

size
integer or null <int64>

The maximum number of items in a page that will be returned.

sort
string or null

The fields by which the returned data will be sorted. Specified as a csv list of fields. See endpoint description for details of supported fields for sorting.

order
string or null

The sort order for the returned data. Can be either ASC for ascending or DESC for descending.

start
string or null

Cursor for pagination; typically encoded as a string understood by the endpoint.

Responses

evaluation service

https://tempurl.com/v1/customers/{customer_id}/legal-entities/{legal_entity_id}/trading-locations

    • "draft": {

    • "updated": "string",
    • "updated_by": "string"
    }
]

CREATE

Creates a new Trading Location for the specified Customer and Legal Entity. A trading location represents a place (physical or online) where the business conducts trading activity.

Purpose

Trading locations provide structured, versioned records of:

  • Where the business operates (shops, sites, online channels, etc.)
  • How those locations are identified and described operationally
  • Which Legal Entity is responsible for activity at each location

They are a key part of onboarding and risk evaluation, and are used by downstream systems for routing, reporting, and compliance checks.

Lifecycle & Versions

Trading locations follow the standard entity lifecycle:

  • draft, submitted, and approved version numbers all begin at 0.
  • The draft copy is populated with the data from the request body.

If submit=true is provided:

  • The draft data is also copied to the submitted slot.
  • The trading location is immediately submitted for evaluation.

If submit is not set:

  • The trading location remains in draft form until explicitly submitted via the submit endpoint or via a Legal Entity–driven workflow.

The approved slot is only updated once evaluation completes successfully and the apply workflow has run.

The trading location is created with its legal_entity_id set from the URL; this association cannot be changed after creation.

Submission & Evaluation

Submission may be triggered:

  • Directly by calling this endpoint with submit=true, or
  • Indirectly from a Legal Entity workflow that includes one or more trading locations as part of a wider change set.

During evaluation, the system may identify:

  • Missing or invalid address / location details
  • Conflicts or inconsistencies with the Legal Entity configuration
  • Additional document or data requirements

Any findings are returned to the caller and must be resolved before the approved copy is updated.

Validation & Authorisation

  • Caller must have permission to CREATE_TRADING_LOCATION for the target customer and legal entity.
  • Authorisation is provided via a signed JWT issued by the authorisation server.
  • The request payload must pass validation; failures are returned with structured error details.

Result

Returns the newly created Trading Location wrapped in an EntityResponse<TradingLocation> with a 201 Created status.

Authorizations:
oauth2
path Parameters
customer_id
required
string

Id of the customer for the operation

legal_entity_id
required
string

Id of the legal entity

query Parameters
submit
required
boolean

flag to indicate if the trading location should be immediately submitted

Request Body schema: application/json
required
null or Address (object)
required
Array of objects (TradingLocationContact)

A list of contacts associated with the trading location

name
required
string

Name used to refer to this location

website
string or null
Default: "https://example.com"

The website url (eg https://my.domain.con) of the trading location (either postal address or website must be specified)

Responses

evaluation service

https://tempurl.com/v1/customers/{customer_id}/legal-entities/{legal_entity_id}/trading-locations

      • "contact_id": "string",
      • "contact_type": "Manager"
      }
    ],
  • "name": "string",
  • "website": "https://example.com"
}

GET

Retrieves a specific Trading Location by ID.

Purpose

Display the current draft/submitted/approved state for a location before editing or submission.

Behaviour

  • Requires READ_TRADING_LOCATION.
  • Returns 404 when the location is not found or not visible to the caller.
  • Includes version/status context in EntityResponse<TradingLocation>.
Authorizations:
oauth2
path Parameters
customer_id
required
string

Id of the customer for the operation

legal_entity_id
required
string

Id of the legal entity

entity_id
required
string

Id of the trading location

Responses

evaluation service

https://tempurl.com/v1/customers/{customer_id}/legal-entities/{legal_entity_id}/trading-locations/{entity_id}

UPDATE

Updates an existing Trading Location draft.

Purpose

Refine location details before submission or after evaluation feedback.

Behaviour

  • Requires UPDATE_TRADING_LOCATION.
  • Uses optimistic concurrency via EntityUpdateRequest.version.
  • Optional submit flag copies the change to the submitted slot and triggers evaluation.
  • Authorisation scoped to customer and legal entity.
Authorizations:
oauth2
path Parameters
customer_id
required
string

Id of the customer for the operation

legal_entity_id
required
string

Id of the legal entity

entity_id
required
string

Id of the item to be updated

query Parameters
submit
required
boolean

flag to indicate if the trading location change should be immediately submitted

Request Body schema: application/json
required
null or Address (object)
Array of objects or null (TradingLocationContact)
name
required
string
website
string or null
Default: "https://example.com"

Responses

evaluation service

https://tempurl.com/v1/customers/{customer_id}/legal-entities/{legal_entity_id}/trading-locations/{entity_id}

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
application/json
{
  • "address": { },
  • "contacts": [
    • {
      • "contact_id": "string",
      • "contact_type": "Manager"
      }
    ],
  • "name": "string",
  • "website": "https://example.com"
}

  • 200
Content type
application/json
{
  • "count": 0,
  • "version": 0
}

DELETE

Deletes an existing Trading Location.

Purpose

Remove obsolete or erroneous locations prior to activation or usage in downstream processes.

Behaviour

  • Requires DELETE_TRADING_LOCATION.
  • Returns 200 on successful deletion; 404 if the record does not exist.
  • Authorisation scoped to the customer and legal entity.
Authorizations:
oauth2
path Parameters
customer_id
required
string

Id of the customer for the operation

legal_entity_id
required
string

Id of the legal entity

entity_id
required
string

Id of the trading location

Responses

evaluation service

https://tempurl.com/v1/customers/{customer_id}/legal-entities/{legal_entity_id}/trading-locations/{entity_id}

SUBMIT

Submits the current draft of a Trading Location for evaluation.

Purpose

Trigger validation of the location (address, operations, associations) prior to activation.

Behaviour

  • Requires SUBMIT_TRADING_LOCATION.
  • Copies draft data to the submitted slot; approved data is unchanged until evaluation/apply completes.
  • Returns 202 Accepted with submission metadata.
  • Authorisation scoped to the customer and legal entity.
Authorizations:
oauth2
path Parameters
customer_id
required
string

Id of the customer for the operation

legal_entity_id
required
string

Id of the legal entity for which the trading locations should be returned

entity_id
required
string

Id of the trading location for the operation

Request Body schema: application/json
required

Details about the draft that should be submitted for approval.

draft_version
required
integer <int64>
reason
string or null

Responses

evaluation service

https://tempurl.com/v1/customers/{customer_id}/legal-entities/{legal_entity_id}/trading-locations/{entity_id}/submit

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
application/json
{
  • "draft_version": 0,
  • "reason": "string"
}

Response samples

  • 202
Content type
application/json
{
  • "entity_id": "string",
  • "submitted_version": 0
}

TOUCH

Triggers re-evaluation for the specified Trading Location.

Purpose

Manually start the evaluation workflow (e.g., after external data changes) without submitting new draft content.

Behaviour

  • Requires EVALUATE_TRADING_LOCATION.
  • Invokes service-level evaluation for the customer/legal entity context.
  • Returns 200 once the evaluation request is queued/processed.
Authorizations:
oauth2
path Parameters
customer_id
required
string

Id of the customer for the operation

legal_entity_id
required
string

Id of the legal entity

entity_id
required
string

Id of the entity for the operation

Responses

evaluation service

https://tempurl.com/v1/customers/{customer_id}/legal-entities/{legal_entity_id}/trading-locations/{entity_id}/touch
